collection_date: Over 12 million registered as of February 2025
Women hold 14% of mobile money accounts

original_text: First major mobile money service in Ethiopia
Historic: digital > cash for first time

source_name: World Bank Global Findex 2025 (via Birr Metrics)
original_text: 36 percent saved in account with 21 percent using digital payments
notes: Fills the DEPTH pillar, which had zero observations. Depth measures inclusion beyond payments (savings/credit) and is needed to forecast whether Usage growth is translating into deeper product use.

source_name: World Bank Global Findex 2025 (via Birr Metrics)
notes: Second DEPTH observation. Very low formal credit penetration is also flagged as a market nuance in the Additional Data Points Guide (sheet D) - worth tracking as its own low baseline.

original_text: by the end of 2023 activity among newly recruited agents had climbed to 42 percent
notes: Fills QUALITY pillar (previously empty). Measures whether the agent network actually functions reliably, not just its registered size. Confidence medium: figure is for newly recruited agents in one operator partnership (Highlight Trading), not verified as a national-level statistic.
Baseline point (FY2019/20) for agent network growth trend, pairs with REC_0035. Physical agent presence is a direct ACCESS enabler per the Additional Data Points Guide (sheet B).

original_text: the number of mobile money agents also rose during this period, increasing by 1,726%, from 22,725 to 415,084
notes: ~18x growth in registered agents FY2019/20-FY2023/24. Counts registrations, not active agents - cross-reference with REC_0033 (42% activity rate) rather than treating this as effective capacity.

original_text: Access to electricity (% of population) in Ethiopia was reported at 55.4 % in 2023
notes: Indirect/enabling correlate per Additional Data Points Guide sheet C - device charging, agent point uptime, and POS/network reliability depend on grid access. Assigned to ACCESS as the closest pillar since it's a physical-reach enabler, though it is a proxy variable, not a direct financial-inclusion measure.

original_text: an initiative to bring clean and reliable electricity to nearly six million people in Ethiopia
notes: Pillar deliberately left empty per schema. $424M program ($400M IDA credit + $24M Denmark grant); relevant as an ACCESS-enabling event via electricity, not yet linked to any indicator - see IMP_0018/IMP_0017.

source_name: National Bank of Ethiopia Financial Stability Report (via Capital Market Ethiopia)
original_text: Twenty-eight Ethiopian banks have reported combined losses of 1.3 billion birr to fraud in the financial year ending June 2024
notes: Fills TRUST pillar - previously zero observations. 28 of Ethiopia's 31 banks were affected. Rising fraud is a direct headwind against trust-driven usage growth and should be modeled as a constraining factor, not just background context.

source_name: National Bank of Ethiopia (via The Reporter Ethiopia)
original_text: NBE reported 4,623 cyberattack attempts in the first half of 2024, a 115 percent increase compared to the same period in 2023
notes: Second TRUST observation. Cited directly in NBE's draft second National Digital Payments Strategy (NDPS 2.0) as a key risk to the sector's trust and stability.

indicator: Mobile Money Account Active Rate (National, All Providers)
source_name: NBE draft National Digital Payments Strategy 2.0
original_text: the document indicates that although Ethiopia boasts a total of nearly 140 million mobile money accounts, only 15 percent of them are active
notes: IMPORTANT: this is a different metric from REC_0025 (USG_ACTIVE_RATE=66%, 2024-12-31), which likely reflects a single platform's 90-day active rate. This figure is NBE's national, all-provider count (~140M registered accounts, cross-provider). Do not treat these as contradictory - they measure different scopes. Confidence medium since it's from a draft strategy document, not a finalized report.

indicator: Adults Lacking Mobile Money Skills - Female
source_name: NBE draft National Digital Payments Strategy 2.0
original_text: Low digital literacy: 66% of women and 60% of men lack mobile-money skills
notes: New GENDER indicator (digital skills, not just account ownership). Pairs with REC_0041 (male figure) to give a 6pp skills gap - a specific, actionable barrier distinct from the existing GEN_GAP_ACC ownership gap.

indicator: Adults Lacking Mobile Money Skills - Male
source_name: NBE draft National Digital Payments Strategy 2.0
original_text: Low digital literacy: 66% of women and 60% of men lack mobile-money skills
notes: Male counterpart to REC_0040.

indicator: Mobile Broadband Basket Cost (% of Monthly GNI per capita)
original_text: In 2017, they still represented nearly 12% of GNI
notes: Historical baseline for the AFFORDABILITY trend, same indicator_code as the existing 2024 point (REC_0026=2%). Confidence medium: source states 'nearly 12%', an approximation rather than an exact figure.

indicator: Mobile Broadband Basket Cost (% of Monthly GNI per capita)
source_name: British International Investment (BII), citing ITU DataHub
original_text: In Ethiopia in 2022, the mobile data-only basket was close to the UN's target at only 3.4 per cent of monthly GNI per capita
notes: Midpoint of the AFFORDABILITY trend: 2017 (~12%) -> 2022 (3.4%) -> 2024 (2%, already in dataset as REC_0026). Now a proper 3-point time series instead of a single snapshot - directly usable to model the affordability driver behind Usage growth.

source_name: NBE Financial Stability Report (via Birr Metrics)
original_text: The gross cost of bank fraud and forgeries increased from one billion birr in 2022/23
notes: Prior-year point for REC_0037 (1.3bn birr, FY2023/24). Now a real 2-point TRUST trend showing a 30% YoY rise in fraud losses, not just a single snapshot.

original_text: At baseline in 2022, just 17 percent of agents were active
notes: 2022 baseline for REC_0046 (42%, end of 2023) — same source, same program. Turns QUALITY from a single number into a real trend: activity rate roughly 2.5x'd in one year, worth flagging as a program-specific effect rather than a national trend given both points are from the same pilot (Highlight Trading).

source_name: Wikipedia (aggregating BBC, Euronews, Addis Standard reporting)
original_text: Ethiopia's state bank system compromised for hours; loss undisclosed... millions of birr withdrawn due to internal glitch
notes: Pillar deliberately left empty per schema. A system malfunction at Ethiopia's largest bank (CBE) let customers withdraw far more than their balances for several hours before NBE/CBE contained it. Directly relevant to TRUST and QUALITY — linked via IMP_0020.

original_text: 26% of adults save at financial institutions (as compared to 14% in 2014)
notes: 2014 baseline for DEPTH_SAVE_RATE, pairs with REC_0050 (2017) and REC_0044 (2024=36%). Now a real 3-point trend: 14%->26%->36%.

original_text: 26% of adults save at financial institutions (as compared to 14% in 2014)
notes: 2017 midpoint for DEPTH_SAVE_RATE trend.

original_text: 11% borrow from financial institutions (as compared to 7% in 2014)
notes: 2014 baseline for DEPTH_BORROW_RATE, pairs with REC_0052 (2017) and REC_0045 (2024=4%). Trend is non-monotonic (7%->11%->4%) - worth investigating why formal borrowing fell by 2024, possibly a real finding for the impact model.

original_text: 11% borrow from financial institutions (as compared to 7% in 2014)
notes: 2017 midpoint for DEPTH_BORROW_RATE trend.

original_text: The highest gap is observed in formal account holding, which amounts to 16.3%
notes: 2017 point for GEN_GAP_ACC, pairs with existing REC_0027 (2021=20pp) and REC_0028 (2024=18pp). Trend is non-monotonic (16.3->20->18) - gap widened before narrowing, consistent with the CGAP finding that Ethiopia 'had yet to see important gains from mobile money' as of 2017.

original_text: followed by borrowing from a formal financial institution with a gender gap of 9.8%
notes: New GENDER indicator - gap specifically in formal credit access, distinct from account ownership gap. Directly relevant to DEPTH_BORROW_RATE (REC_0045/51/52) as a disaggregation.

original_text: 57% of men had a phone, compared to 46% of women
notes: 2022 point for GEN_GAP_MOBILE, pairs with existing REC_0030 (2024=24pp). Shows the mobile-ownership gender gap roughly doubled 2022->2024 (11pp->24pp) - a concerning divergence worth flagging as a real finding, not just a data addition: device access may be a growing (not shrinking) barrier for women even as overall access expands.

source_name: Ethiopia Ministry of Justice report (via Fana Broadcasting / Ethiopian Monitor)
original_text: the banking industry's fraud losses at 1.8 billion birr...over four years, with CBE shouldering nearly half
notes: Deliberately a SEPARATE indicator_code from TRUST_FRAUD_LOSS (REC_0037/47), not a third point in that series - this figure is a 4-year cumulative total (~2018-2022) from a Ministry of Justice probe, not an annual NBE Financial Stability Report figure. Mixing the two would misleadingly suggest fraud losses dropped from 1.8bn to 1.0bn. Useful as an earlier-period reference point, not as a continuation of the annual trend.
